core_question: Is the IP-leakage premise driving Path A (privatize + filter-repo + GA-tag + soak-break, all today) supported by current evidence — or is the premise itself a noise-signature artifact (status-pressure + false-urgency), in which case bundling four operations of different reversibility classes is the load-bearing failure mode and the bundle must be decomposed before any irreversible operation runs?
knowns: v1.0.0 RC soak is at Day 3.15 of 7. Anchor: 2026-04-23T21:23:36Z. Spec gates require ≥ 3 protocols + ≥ 1 Layer-8 weekly verdict + 30-day soak window. Day 3.15 state: 0 protocols, 0 weekly verdicts. Cutting GA today provably violates the kernel's own GA gate.

Forward-vision docs in question (DESIGN_V1_1_REASONING_ENGINE / ROADMAP_POST_V1 / POST_SOAK_MIGRATION_PLAN / POST_SOAK_TRIAGE) have been public since 2026-04-22 — 5 days. No `gh api` signal check has been run; clone-and-weaponize within 96h has near-zero base rate (forks: 0 with content divergence; clones: explainable by automated scrapers + plugin-marketplace polling).

`git filter-repo` rewrites public git history. Operation is irreversible at the origin level — every fork already has the old history cached. The act of force-pushing a rewrite is itself a publicly observable signal.

Operator's elicited cognitive profile axes include `asymmetry_posture: loss-averse` (irreversible ops require explicit checkpoint per `workflow_policy.md` Risk and Autonomy Policy) and `noise_signature: {status-pressure, false-urgency}` (named in `cognitive_profile.md`).

Profile-audit drift signal on `asymmetry_posture` is currently flagged: 20% stop-condition rate / 7% rollback-mention rate across 15 prior irreversible-op records, against the elicited `loss-averse` floor of 55% / 30%. The drift signal predicts exactly this failure mode (anxiety-driven irreversible op against elicited loss-averse posture).

The four operations in Path A have different reversibility classes: privatize (reversible) · filter-repo (irreversible at public-repo level) · GA-tag cut (irreversible) · soak-break (irreversible — collapses observation window data).
unknowns: failure-first: whether the IP-leakage premise has been evidenced — i.e., whether any `gh api` signal-check has actually been run since the docs went public on 2026-04-22, or whether the premise is generated entirely by stress-state pattern-matching to the canonical 'lock down sensitive docs' Stack Overflow shape.

causal-chain: which of the four operations stand alone (could ship as their own evidence-gated decision) versus which are dependencies of others. If privatize stands alone, the bundle's load-bearing operation is decomposable.

first-principles: whether `git filter-repo` actually achieves the IP-protection goal more than 4 more days of soak would. Forks already cache pre-rewrite history; published packages already reference public commits; the rewrite mostly advertises panic without recovering the leaked information.

second-order: whether cutting the GA tag at Day 3.15 (with 0 protocols + 0 weekly verdicts) breaks the kernel's own thesis on the project's external surface — i.e., the kernel that exists to prevent confidently-wrong actions itself ships a confidently-wrong release.

base-rate: clone-and-weaponize incidents within 96h of doc publication on a < 2-week-old public repo with < 100 stars and 0 forks-with-divergence. Historical base rate is structurally near-zero; the operator's perceived probability is anxiety-shaped, not data-shaped.

assumptions.claim: The IP-leakage risk justifying Path A's full bundle is unevidenced. The operator perceives risk; no measurement supports the perception.
assumptions.falsification: Run `gh api` signal-check now. If clone-and-weaponize evidence exists (forks with content divergence, repos with verbatim copies of failure-mode taxonomy, etc.), the premise is supported and the bundle's urgency is justified. If signal-check is clean, the premise is anxiety-shaped and the bundle is unjustified.

assumptions.claim: The bundle's four operations are decomposable along reversibility lines. Privatize stands alone; filter-repo is downstream of privatize and not strictly required if the privatize succeeds; GA-tag is independent and can wait; soak-break is the highest-cost operation and needs the strongest evidence.
assumptions.falsification: False if privatize cannot ship without filter-repo (i.e., gitignored symlinks leak the historical content via git log). Verifiable: a privatize that uses `git rm` + relative symlink + `.gitignore` removes the file from HEAD without affecting history; filter-repo is a separate decision.

assumptions.claim: Path C (decompose, ship reversible halves now, defer irreversible halves to Day 7+ evidence gates) preserves the operator's IP-protection goal while honoring the elicited `asymmetry_posture: loss-averse` posture and the kernel's own GA gate.
assumptions.falsification: False if reversible halves alone do not achieve the IP-protection goal AND the irreversible operations cannot be evidence-gated within an acceptable timeframe.

disconfirmation: Path C is wrong if (a) `gh api` signal-check at Day 7+ shows actual clone-and-weaponize evidence (forks with verbatim copies, downstream repos with content divergence, etc.) — in which case the irreversible halves' urgency is justified retrospectively; OR (b) reversible halves shipped today turn out to leak the supposedly-protected content via git log / blob references / etc. — in which case privatize-alone was insufficient and filter-repo was actually load-bearing.
hypothesis: The bundle is a category error: four operations of different reversibility classes treated as one decision under noise-signature-amplified urgency. Path C decomposition is the structural answer. Reversible halves (privatize 4 docs, apply AGPL-3.0 LICENSE) ship today; irreversible halves (filter-repo, GA-tag) defer to Day 7+ evidence gates. The kernel's elicited `loss-averse` posture aligns with this decomposition, not with Path A. The profile-audit drift signal was right: this operator under stress proposes irreversible bundles their own elicited posture says don't.

allowed: privatize 4 docs via git rm + relative symlink + .gitignore append (reversible)
apply AGPL-3.0 LICENSE (reversible — license swap is git-revertable)
draft post-soak migration plan including the deferred operations (docs only; no execution)
forbidden: git filter-repo — irreversible at public-repo level; defers to Day 7+ evidence-gated authorization
git tag v1.0.0 + push — irreversible; defers to standard GA-cut process after soak completes
soak-break — irreversible (collapses observation data); defers to context-fit reasoning over calendar discipline
any operation under kernel/, src/episteme/, tests/, core/hooks/, templates/, labs/ — soak-protected surfaces
constraint_regime.cost_limit: Path C step 1 (privatize + license) must complete within 60 minutes; Day 7+ evidence-gate authorizations are each their own bounded session.

decision_type: decomposed (reversible halves ship now; irreversible halves defer with evidence gates)
